text.title: Security Testing Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Security Testing methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: SAST MUST run on every PR via the appropriate tool per language (semgrep all, bandit Python, gosec Go, brakeman Rails); quarterly-only SAST is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Quarterly SAST catches issues at end of cycle when fix cost is high; per-PR catches at write time.

rule.statement: Dependency audit MUST cover transitive deps via osv.dev + ecosystem audit (npm audit / pip-audit / cargo audit / govulncheck); direct-only audit is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Transitive deps cause 80%+ of supply-chain incidents; direct-only audit misses them entirely.

rule.statement: DAST scans MUST cover authenticated paths via ZAP context with valid session; unauth-only scans are insufficient.
rule.rationale: Auth-protected paths host most CRUD + business logic; unauth-only scan misses the bulk of attack surface.

rule.statement: Secrets scanning MUST run pre-commit (gitleaks / trufflehog) AND in CI; CI-only scanning is rejected.

rationale.source: Git history audit; rotated-key cost analysis

rule.rationale: Pre-commit blocks the secret from entering history; CI-only requires rotation of any committed secret.

rule.statement: Each release MUST publish an ASVS L1 coverage matrix listing covered, in-progress, and accepted-risk controls with rationale + owner.
rule.rationale: Implicit coverage hides gaps; an explicit matrix exposes them for triage and budget.

rule.statement: Findings MUST have a documented fix window: critical 24h, high 7d, medium 30d, low backlog; missing windows are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without windows, severity loses meaning; tracked SLAs surface accumulating risk.

rule.statement: DAST scans MUST respect production rate limits (use scan account in dedicated tier OR scan staging with limits matching prod); blind scans against prod are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Blind scans trigger rate limits + alerts; scans can DoS production by hammering endpoints in parallel.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec - decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
